messages.content: I want a free photos that it's size is 1920 * 1080 can you give me some websites that can provide them for me

Sure, here are a few websites where you can find free photos in the size you mentioned:

1. Pixabay - www.pixabay.com
2. Unsplash - www.unsplash.com
3. Pexels - www.pexels.com
4. Burst by Shopify - www.burst.shopify.com
5. StockSnap - www.stocksnap.io
6. Freepik - www.freepik.com (look for the "Free Photos" section)
7. Gratisography - www.gratisography.com

Remember to check the licensing and usage terms for each website to ensure you are using the photos appropriately.
how can I get a wavy background I want it to be so simple

To create a simple wavy background, you can follow these steps using a graphic design software like Adobe Photoshop or an online design tool like Canva:

1. Open the design tool or software of your choice and create a new document with the dimensions you want (1920 * 1080 pixels).
2. Choose a background color for your design. You can select a solid color or a gradient.
3. Create a new layer on top of the background layer.
4. Select the Pen Tool (or any other drawing tool) to draw a wavy line across the canvas. Make sure the wavy line is smooth and simple.
5. Adjust the color and stroke properties of the wavy line according to your preference.
6. Duplicate the wavy line layer and modify the duplicate by resizing or rotating it to create additional waves, if desired.
7. Continue duplicating and modifying the wavy line layers until you achieve the desired look.
8. Once you are satisfied with the wavy background, save the image in the desired format (e.g., JPEG, PNG).

Alternatively, you can search for pre-made wavy backgrounds or templates on graphic design platforms like Adobe Stock, Shutterstock, or Freepik, and customize them to fit your needs.

I want website that helps me to get some good colors for my app I need colors that fit with each others

Sure! Here are a few websites that can help you find good color combinations that fit well together for your app:

1. Coolors (https://coolors.co/) - Offers a color generator that creates cohesive color schemes with options to refine and customize them.

2. Adobe Color (https://color.adobe.com/) - Provides a wide range of color schemes, including popular and trending palettes, along with customization options.

3. Color Hunt (https://colorhunt.co/) - Curated collection of color palettes created by designers, which you can browse and use in your app.

4. Paletton (https://paletton.com/) - Allows you to create custom color combinations based on the color wheel and offers live preview options to see how the colors work together.

5. Happy Hues (https://www.happyhues.co/) - Showcases ready-to-use color palettes designed specifically for UI and web design.

6. Material UI Colors (https://www.materialui.co/colors) - Offers an extensive collection of color palettes inspired by Google's Material Design guidelines.

These websites should provide you with a range of color options and combinations to choose from, making it easier to find colors that work well together for your app design.
what does Clear do in CSS I mean like Clear:both;

In CSS, the `clear` property is used to control the behavior of a container element when floating elements are present within it. When applied to an element, the `clear` property specifies whether the element can be positioned alongside preceding floating elements or must be moved below them.

The `clear` property accepts the following values:

1. `none` (default): No clearance is applied. The element can be positioned alongside preceding floating elements.
2. `left`: The element is pushed below any preceding left-floating elements.
3. `right`: The element is pushed below any preceding right-floating elements.
4. `both`: The element is pushed below any preceding floating elements, regardless of their alignment (left or right).

Using `clear:both;` means that the element should be moved below any preceding floating elements, regardless of their alignment (left or right). This is particularly useful when you want an element to start on a new line and ensure that it doesn't wrap around any preceding floating elements.
z index isn't applied in the css why does that happen

If the `z-index` property is not being applied as expected in your CSS, there are a few common reasons for this issue:

1. Positioned or Float Elements: `z-index` only works on positioned elements (those with a `position` value set to `relative`, `absolute`, or `fixed`), or elements with a `float` value. Make sure the element you are trying to apply `z-index` to meets these criteria.

2. Parent Element: The `z-index` property is relative to the stacking context of the parent element. If the parent element doesn't have a `position` value set (i.e., `position: relative;`, `position: absolute;`, `position: fixed;`), then the `z-index` property of the child element will not have any effect.

3. Overlapping Context: If the overlapping elements have different stacking contexts due to their parent elements, the `z-index` may not work as expected. Review the parent-child relationships to ensure that the stacking contexts are set up correctly.

4. Auto Stacking: If `z-index` is not explicitly set on elements, their stacking order is determined by their position in the HTML markup. Elements that come later in the markup will be stacked on top of elements located earlier. You may need to adjust the order of your elements in the HTML markup or explicitly define `z-index` values to achieve the desired stacking order.

5. Limited Stacking: If an element has a `z-index` value set, but its parent or sibling elements have a lower `z-index` value or no specified `z-index`, it can prevent the higher `z-index` value from taking effect.

By considering these factors and adjusting the positioning and stacking context of elements, you should be able to resolve any issues with the `z-index` property not being applied as intended.

A ship 140 m long has a rectangular cross section of 20 m beam and 13 m depth. The total
weight of the ship is 25830 tonnes, 20830 tonnes of which are uniformly distributed over the
entire length of the ship; the remaining 5000 tonnes are uniformly distributed over a length of 10
m with its centre of gravity amidships. Calculate the still water bending moment. and hence
repeat the same problem distributing the 5000 tonnes over two intervals each of 5 m, one of
them fore and the other is aft. and compare between the two conditions.

To calculate the still water bending moment for the given ship, we will use the formula:

Bending Moment = Weight x Distance x 0.5

First, let's calculate the bending moment for the initial condition, where the 5000 tonnes is uniformly distributed over a length of 10 m with its center of gravity amidships:

Weight (initial condition) = 5000 tonnes = 5,000,000 kg
Distance (initial condition) = 10 m

Bending Moment (initial condition) = Weight x Distance x 0.5
Bending Moment (initial condition) = 5,000,000 kg x 10 m x 0.5
Bending Moment (initial condition) = 25,000,000 kgm

Now, let's calculate the bending moment when the 5000 tonnes is distributed over two intervals, each of 5 m, one fore and the other aft:

Weight (new condition) = 5000 tonnes = 5,000,000 kg
Distance (new condition) = (5 m - (-5 m)) / 2 = 5 m (distance from the midship to either end)

Bending Moment (new condition) = 2 x (Weight / 2) x Distance x 0.5
Bending Moment (new condition) = 2 x (5,000,000 kg / 2) x 5 m x 0.5
Bending Moment (new condition) = 25,000,000 kgm

As we can see, the bending moment for both the initial and new conditions is the same, which is 25,000,000 kgm. The distribution of weight along the length of the ship does not affect the still water bending moment in this particular case.
how can I prevent certain sites from working in my network

To prevent certain sites from working on your network, you can use various methods, including:

1. Blocking via Router or Firewall: Access your router or network firewall settings and configure it to block specific websites. This method typically involves setting up URL filtering or blacklisting specific domain names or IP addresses.

2. Using Hosts File: Modify the hosts file on individual computers or devices within your network to block certain websites. By adding entries that redirect or block the desired sites, you can effectively prevent access to those sites on those specific devices.

3. DNS Filtering: Use a DNS filtering service or set up a local DNS server that blocks specific domains or websites. DNS filtering works by redirecting requests for blocked sites to a different IP address or by returning an error message.

4. Parental Control Software: Install and configure parental control software on devices within your network. These tools often include features to block specific websites based on customizable filters and rules.

5. Proxy Servers: Configure your network to route internet traffic through a proxy server that allows filtering and blocking of specific websites or content categories.

It's essential to note that implementing these methods may require technical expertise or administrative access to network devices. Also, keep in mind that some users may find workarounds or alternative methods to access blocked sites. For comprehensive network-level blocking, consider using professional systems or consulting with IT professionals for more advanced solutions.
what is the easiest way

The easiest way to block certain websites on your network is by using parental control software or tools. These tools are specifically designed to help block and filter content, making it easier for you to restrict access to specific websites.

Here are a few popular parental control tools/software that are user-friendly and offer website blocking features:

1. OpenDNS FamilyShield: OpenDNS provides an easy-to-use DNS-based content filtering service that allows you to block websites by configuring your network settings. FamilyShield is a pre-configured version of OpenDNS that blocks adult content automatically.

2. Norton Family: Norton Family is a comprehensive parental control software that includes website blocking features. It allows you to create custom web-filtering rules and block access to specific websites based on categories or individual URLs.

3. Kaspersky Safe Kids: Kaspersky Safe Kids is another parental control tool that offers website blocking capabilities. It enables you to block specific websites or categories, track browser history, and set time limits for internet usage.

4. Qustodio: Qustodio is a user-friendly parental control software that provides features such as website filtering, time controls, and activity monitoring. It allows you to create custom profiles for different users and block access to inappropriate websites.

These tools typically offer intuitive user interfaces and straightforward setup processes, making them the easiest way to block websites on your network. However, keep in mind that some of these tools may have feature limitations or require a subscription for complete functionality.
